title: senecio richardsonii
intro: Senecio richardsonii, also known as Richardson's groundsel or butterweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the western parts of North America and has been used by various indigenous cultures for its medicinal and therapeutic properties. The plant's roots and leaves contain alkaloids, such as jervine and senecionine, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Senecio richardsonii can be prepared as an herbal remedy in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, offering potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, although its use should be carefully considered due to potential toxicity.
